suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Enforce the completion gate more strictly: require the final diff review to reject package/manifest version bumps before workers report `followedProcedure: true`, and make later cleanup/rewrite requirements explicit when a worker's original commit violates that rule.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The round 5 re-review for `fix-providerinstance-authoritative-base` passed only after a later history rewrite moved the real code change to clean commit `1d7c344` and isolated version metadata in `7c6cb22`; the original worker handoff for obsolete commit `74ad48b` still reported `followedProcedure: true` even though that commit bundled forbidden version bumps.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Validate recorded commit IDs with `git rev-parse` before persisting worker handoffs so scrutiny reviewers can inspect the exact commit without manual repair.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `fix-version-bump-isolation` handoff stored malformed full hash `b704036ed978544f7d9d4d09f0fb15a2b558a6e4`; review had to recover the real commit with `git rev-parse b704036`, which resolved to `b70403648b11f779c8a845d4b7d86d39693d90f2`.
